summary: Ukrainian forces captured a Ukrainian Armed Forces "Shark-M" fixed-wing drone after its parachute deployed following a kinetic intercept by the Russian "Yolka" air defense drone in the Krasny Liman area, with minimal damage to the UAV. Russian operators from the "Rubicon" Center reported destroying 129 Ukrainian targets on April 18, 2026, including personnel and fixed-wing UAVs across the Donbas, Krasny Liman, Belgorod, and Sumy directions. Ukrainian forces employed aerial relay drones to extend FPV strike ranges to 15-20 km, bypassing electronic warfare interference, while both sides used FPV drones for close-range attacks, including targeting soldiers on motorcycles and exploiting surrender feints.
